name: Efficient Properties Specification Workflow v4.0
description:
Builds on v3.9 with FP-prevention improvements from Morpho/Enzyme/Radiant analysis.

Key additions in v4.0:
- BeforeAfter template: actor consistency tracking (_beforeActor, _afterActor, _actorConsistent)
- Properties template: address(0) guard, tolerance derivation, stale-vs-live, delegation tracking comments
- Phase 0: Setup liveness validation report (contract != address(0) checks, target function revert rate prediction)
- Phase 1A: PRIV-NEG tier (privilege escalation negative tests), PATTERN 25 (share inflation), PATTERN 26 (math round-trip), PATTERN 23-CROSS-D (deposit-withdraw sandwich)
- Phase 2: NEVER_DISCARD guardrail, AP-9 (core-read-masking), AP-10 (stale/live mismatch), AP-11 (compound op ghost invalidation), AP-12 (rounding direction conflict), gateway/proxy modifier mismatch audit
- Phase 3A: ACTOR_GHOST_GUARD, ADDRESS_ZERO_GUARD, TOLERANCE_JUSTIFICATION, DELEGATION_TRACKING, STALE_LIVE_CONSISTENCY mandatory rules; ProfitTracker delegation-aware implementation; Step 0H admin validation negative tests; core-read-fail rule; ghost variable depth rules
- Workflow: smoke-test-gate (10K test pre-flight before full campaign)
- Workflow: smoke test step after Phase 3B to catch shallow FPs before coverage

Changes from v3.8/v3.9:
- Phase 0 agent: v3.8 -> v4 (added Step 9: Setup Liveness Validation)
- Phase 1A agent: v3.7 -> v4 (added PRIV-NEG, share inflation, math round-trip, sandwich patterns from v3.9)
- Phase 2 agent: v3.6 -> v4 (merged v3.9 NEVER_DISCARD + AP-9 + modifier mismatch + v4 AP-10/11/12)
- Phase 3A agent: v3.7 -> v4 (merged v3.9 Step 0H + core-read-fail + ghost depth + v4 mandatory guard rules + delegation-aware ProfitTracker)
- Workflow: added smoke-test-gate + smoke test validation step after Phase 3B
- Phases 1B, 3B: unchanged (1B=v3.4, 3B=v3.4)

steps.name: Phase 0: Dependency + Reachability + Wiring + Taint Analysis + Liveness Validation
steps.description: Analyze contracts for dependencies, review priority, reachability of guarded branches, protocol type classification, economic oracle identification, data-flow taint analysis, Setup.sol wiring issues, and setup liveness validation
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-0-v4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-0 agent using the Task tool. This phase will analyze all contracts, create a dependency list, generate a review priority order, perform a reachability analysis to identify hard-to-reach branches that need shortcut target functions, classify the protocol type (AMM/LENDING/VAULT/ERC20/GOVERNANCE/STAKING), identify economic vulnerability oracles, perform data-flow taint analysis (source/sink identification, taint propagation tracing, exploitability scoring), build a function transition graph (state-enabling relationships, entry/terminal points), analyze Setup.sol for wiring issues (constructor params, proxy patterns, access control, funding), and generate setup liveness validation (contract != address(0) checks, target function revert rate prediction).

steps.name: Validate Dependency List
steps.description: Check if magic/contracts-dependency-list.md was created

decision.NOTE: Dependency list created, continue to next validation
Phase 0 failed to create magic/contracts-dependency-list.md

steps.name: Validate Review Priority
steps.description: Check if magic/properties-review-priority.md was created

decision.NOTE: Review priority created, continue to reachability validation
Phase 0 failed to create magic/properties-review-priority.md

steps.name: Validate Reachability Analysis
steps.description: Check if magic/reachability-analysis.md was created

decision.NOTE: Reachability analysis created, continue to wiring analysis validation
Reachability analysis not created. Phase 1A will proceed without it, but CANARY/DOOM coverage may be weaker.

steps.name: Validate Setup Wiring Analysis
steps.description: Check if magic/setup-wiring-analysis.md was created by Phase 0 Step 5

decision.NOTE: Setup wiring analysis created. Phase 3A will use it to fix Setup.sol before property implementation.
Setup wiring analysis not created. Phase 3A will attempt to detect wiring issues independently, but may miss some.

steps.name: Validate Economic Oracles
steps.description: Check if magic/economic-oracles.md was created by Phase 0 Step 6

decision.NOTE: Economic oracles analysis created. Phase 1A will use it to generate economic vulnerability properties.
Economic oracles analysis not created. Phase 1A will proceed without economic vulnerability mapping. Standard tier generation and protocol-type templates still provide adequate coverage.

steps.name: Validate Dataflow Taint Analysis
steps.description: Check if magic/dataflow-taint-analysis.md was created by Phase 0 Step 7

decision.NOTE: Dataflow taint analysis created. Phase 1A will use it for taint-informed property density modulation.
Dataflow taint analysis not created. Phase 1A will proceed with uniform property density (v3.5 behavior). Taint-informed prioritization will be skipped.

steps.name: Validate Function Transitions
steps.description: Check if magic/function-transitions.json was created by Phase 0 Step 8

decision.NOTE: Function transitions graph created. Full-audit pipeline will use it for pre-fuzzing sequence analysis and coverage iteration dead-cycle analysis.
Function transitions graph not created. Pre-fuzzing sequence analysis and dead-cycle detection will proceed without transition data.

steps.name: Validate Protocol Type Detection
steps.description: Check if PROTOCOL_TYPE classification was added to the dependency list by Phase 0 Step 4d

decision.NOTE: Protocol type detected. Phase 1A will inject protocol-specific property templates.
Protocol type not detected. Phase 1A will skip protocol-specific templates. Standard tier generation still applies.

steps.name: Validate Ghost Infrastructure
steps.description: Check if updateGhosts modifier is applied to target functions. Prerequisite for INLINE properties.

decision.NOTE: updateGhosts found on target functions, INLINE properties will work
updateGhosts may be missing from target functions. Phase 1A will document this as INFRASTRUCTURE_REQUIRED. Phase 3A will fix it.

steps.name: Phase 1A: Tiered Property Identification (Tiers 0-10)
steps.description: Generate high-priority security-relevant properties for Tiers 0-10 (PROFIT through DOOM) using inline mandatory patterns, protocol-type templates, economic oracle integration, taint-informed property prioritization, condensed anti-patterns, and reachability integration
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-1a-v4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-1a agent using the Task tool. This phase will generate properties for Tiers 0-10 (PROFIT oracle, CANARY, solvency, health factor, liquidation, monotonicity, math, variable transitions, exchange rate, fees, doomsday) organized by financial impact priority, with architectural prefixes, attack scenarios, protocol-type templates, cross-protocol templates (locked funds, delegatecall safety, selfdestruct protection), economic oracle integration, expanded economic oracles (flash loan profitability, sandwich resistance), taint-informed property prioritization (density modulation by taint classification: HIGH_TAINT min 4/function, MEDIUM min 2, LOW canary-only, NO skip), privilege escalation negative tests (PRIV-NEG tier), share inflation / first-depositor attack detection (PATTERN 25), math library round-trip inflation (PATTERN 26), and deposit-withdraw sandwich resistance (PATTERN 23-CROSS-D).

steps.name: Validate First Pass Properties
steps.description: Check if magic/properties-efficient-first-pass.md was created by Phase 1A

decision.NOTE: First pass properties created by Phase 1A, continue to Phase 1B
Phase 1A failed to create magic/properties-efficient-first-pass.md

steps.name: Phase 1B: Low-Priority Tiers + Free-Form Discovery
steps.description: Generate properties for Tiers 11-15 and perform free-form discovery pass with fresh context. Reads Phase 1A output to avoid duplicates and fill gaps.
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-1b-v3.4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-1b agent using the Task tool. This phase will generate properties for Tiers 11-15 (state transition, valid state, peripheral, dust, bitmap) and perform a free-form line-by-line review of top contracts to catch protocol-specific edge cases. It reads Phase 1A's output to avoid duplicates.

steps.name: Validate First Pass Appendix
steps.description: Check that Phase 1B appended its properties to the first pass file

decision.NOTE: Phase 1B content found in first pass file. Continue to Phase 2.
Phase 1B header not found in first pass file. Phase 2 will proceed with Phase 1A properties only. Low-priority tiers and free-form discoveries may be missing.

steps.name: Phase 2: Review, Ghost Classification, Stale-Op Analysis, and FP Applicability Patterns
steps.description: Review properties, classify ghost infrastructure, flag STALE_OP_RISK, apply AP-10/11/12 FP patterns, filter invalid properties
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-2-v4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-2 agent using the Task tool. This phase will review and filter properties (including Tier 0 PROFIT), classify all target functions by ghost modifier category, flag stale-operation risks, apply AP-10 (stale/live mismatch), AP-11 (compound op ghost invalidation), AP-12 (rounding direction conflict) FP detection, compute fuzzer appeal scores (5-point difficulty rating with NEEDS_SHORTCUT_HANDLER flagging), and create the refined properties list with an INFRASTRUCTURE STATUS header and APPEAL SCORE DISTRIBUTION.

steps.name: Validate Second Pass Properties
steps.description: Check if magic/properties-efficient-second-pass.md was created

decision.NOTE: Second pass properties created, continue to infrastructure validation
Phase 2 failed to create magic/properties-efficient-second-pass.md

steps.name: Validate Infrastructure Status
steps.description: Check that INFRASTRUCTURE STATUS section was written into second-pass file

decision.NOTE: INFRASTRUCTURE STATUS found. Phase 3A can use it for ghost classification and stale-op guards.
Phase 2 created second-pass but MISSING INFRASTRUCTURE STATUS section. Re-run Phase 2.

steps.name: Validate Stale-Op Classification
steps.description: Check that target function classification table was included

decision.NOTE: Target function classification found. Phase 3A can determine if Category B functions exist.
Target function classification missing. Phase 3A will re-derive it from TargetFunctions.sol directly.

steps.name: Check Recon Setup Exists
steps.description: Check if recon/Setup.sol exists before proceeding to property implementation

decision.NOTE: Recon Setup found, continue to Phase 3A implementation
Recon Setup not found - run recon-generate first to create scaffolding

steps.name: Phase 3A: Wiring + Infrastructure + ProfitTracker + Handler Templates + SIMPLE/CANARY Implementation + v4 Guards
steps.description: Fix Setup.sol wiring (Step -1), ghost infrastructure (Step 0), delegation-aware ProfitTracker ghost contract (Step 0F), protocol-aware handler templates (Step 0G), implement SIMPLE + CANARY properties with v4 mandatory guards (actor consistency, address(0), tolerance justification, delegation tracking, stale-live consistency). Verifies compilation before Phase 3B.
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-3a-v4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-3a agent using the Task tool. This phase will first fix Setup.sol wiring (constructor params, proxy implementations, modifier selection, funding, protocol config), then fix ghost infrastructure (updateGhosts on target functions, reset fix for Category B), implement delegation-aware ProfitTracker ghost contract (ProtocolSnapshot struct, cumulative sequence tracking, PROFIT-04/05 properties, onBehalfOf/delegation-aware tracking), generate protocol-aware handler templates (ERC20/VAULT/AMM/LENDING/STAKING attack-pattern handlers), implement PROFIT oracle infrastructure (_initialBalances tracking), implement all SIMPLE properties (PROFIT, SOL, MON, MATH, ER, VS) and CANARY properties, apply v4 mandatory rules (ACTOR_GHOST_GUARD, ADDRESS_ZERO_GUARD, TOLERANCE_JUSTIFICATION, DELEGATION_TRACKING, STALE_LIVE_CONSISTENCY), then run forge build and forge test to verify compilation and setUp().

steps.name: Validate Phase 3A Compilation
steps.description: Verify Phase 3A code compiles before proceeding to Phase 3B

decision.NOTE: Phase 3A compilation succeeded. SIMPLE + CANARY properties compile cleanly. Continue to Phase 3B.
Phase 3A compilation failed. Fix compilation errors before proceeding to Phase 3B.

steps.name: Validate Phase 3A setUp
steps.description: Verify setUp() doesn't revert after Phase 3A wiring changes

decision.NOTE: Tests ran (setUp didn't revert). Continue to Phase 3B.
Could not verify setUp(). Phase 3B will proceed but may encounter runtime errors.

steps.name: Phase 3B: INLINE + NEGATIVE + DOOM Implementation
steps.description: Implement INLINE, NEGATIVE, and DOOMSDAY properties building on Phase 3A's verified infrastructure.
steps.prompt: You are provided an agent definition at ./.opencode/agent/properties-phase-3b-v3.4.md. Run exclusively the properties-phase-3b agent using the Task tool. This phase will implement INLINE properties (VT, FEE, ST, PERIPH), NEGATIVE properties (HF, LIQ, VT-NEG, PRIV-NEG privilege escalation tests), and DOOMSDAY properties (DOOM) using the verified infrastructure from Phase 3A.

steps.name: Validate Phase 3B Compilation
steps.description: Verify final compilation after all properties are implemented

decision.NOTE: Full property suite compiles. Continue to smoke test.
Phase 3B compilation failed. Check magic/properties-blocker.md for unresolvable issues.

steps.name: Smoke Test: Validate Properties Against Fuzzer
steps.description: Run 10K-test campaign to catch shallow FPs before handing off to coverage workflow
steps.prompt: The smoke test gate has validated that no shallow false positives exist. Review magic/smoke-test-output.log if it exists and confirm all properties pass at 10K tests. If any failures were fixed by the gate's fix step, document them in magic/coded-properties.md under a 'Smoke Test Fixes' section.
